title: Ordinal learning for emotion recognition in customer service calls

review_output.candidate_title: Ordinal learning for emotion recognition in customer service calls

source_record_provenance.record_title: Ordinal learning for emotion recognition in customer service calls

satisfied_inclusion_points: Paper pertains to preference learning / ordinal learning in audio domain and uses a CORAL-based approach to model ordinal emotions (K-1 binary tasks).
Experimentation is in the audio domain (speech emotion recognition on call-center data and IEMOCAP).
Not a survey or review; presents original method, encoding, training, and evaluation evidence.
evidence_highlights: Uses CORAL to reform ordinal emotion labels into K-1 binary tasks, ensuring rank-monotonic predictions.
Applies VGGish-CORAL with task importance weighting to handle data imbalance and improve performance on audio corpora.
Demonstrates evaluation on real-world call-center data and the IEMOCAP corpus with improved metrics over baseline.

No explicit exclusion criteria are triggered based on the provided text.

evidence_highlights: The work focuses on ranking speech samples according to expressive content using a preference-learning framework.

MSP-Podcast corpus is used for experiments, with multiple annotators (at least five) and audio-focused attributes (arousal, valence, dominance).

Concludes that anchor-based ordinal labels improve preference learning performance in audio emotion recognition.
review_output.decision_rationale: The submission clearly meets Stage 2 inclusion criteria: it addresses preference learning within the audio domain (speech emotion), describes a learning methodology (anchor-based ordinal labels, RankNet), and provides empirical evaluation showing improved performance over baseline labeling strategies. title: Emo-dpo: Controllable emotional speech synthesis through direct preference optimization

review_output.candidate_title: Emo-dpo: Controllable emotional speech synthesis through direct preference optimization

source_record_provenance.record_title: Emo-dpo: Controllable emotional speech synthesis through direct preference optimization

satisfied_inclusion_points: Paper pertains to preference learning, defined as ranking or A/B comparison of two or more audio clips; the work uses pairwise preference data and a Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) training objective.
Paper is applied within the audio domain (emotional speech synthesis) and includes an emotion-aware LLM-TTS architecture, satisfying the audio-domain requirement.
The paper is not a survey or review; it presents a concrete methodology (instruction tuning + DPO training) and empirical evaluations.
evidence_highlights: Employs Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) with pairwise comparisons to align outputs with preferred emotions.
Introduces instruction tuning and emotion-aware LLM-TTS to generate emotion-specific speech tokens.
JS-divergence regularization and joint loss design used to stabilize training.
Experiments on an emotion-rich English speech dataset show Emo-DPO outperforms baselines in emotion similarity and intelligibility metrics.
